# Evidence

- Anthropic, "Effective context engineering for AI agents": subagents are useful because they isolate large search contexts and return short summaries.
- Anthropic, "How we built our multi-agent research system": multi-agent systems can improve results, but they are expensive; vague delegation causes duplicated work.
- The same Anthropic post notes that most coding tasks are not parallelizable enough to justify broad multi-agent decomposition.

Full source list: [shared references](../../REFERENCES.md)
